@eldubuu @sgt1372 I agree that the President is required (per his oath) to faithfully execute *all* the laws.However, as a practical matter that definition allows a lot of wiggle room to an executive who wants to slow walk that "faithful execution".  For instance, Biden (or the Secretary) waived environmental requirements (found in other laws, also passed by Congress) in order to resume this construction. That waiver didn't need to happen - it could have been dragged out for years.
